name: Lanka
description: Lanka, also known as Sri Lanka, is a legendary island city described in Hindu mythology, primarily in the Ramayana. It was the opulent capital of the Rakshasa kingdom, ruled by the ten-headed demon king Ravana. Lanka was renowned for its stunning architecture, constructed of gold, and its advanced infrastructure. The island's strategic location and natural defenses made it a formidable stronghold. The city's significance lies in its pivotal role in the epic Ramayana, where Rama, with the help of Hanuman and his army of monkeys, wages war against Ravana to rescue his wife Sita, who was abducted and held captive in Lanka. The battle between Rama and Ravana symbolizes the eternal struggle between good and evil. The defeat of Ravana and the liberation of Sita from Lanka is a central theme of the Ramayana, representing the triumph of dharma (righteousness) over adharma (unrighteousness). Lanka is not merely a geographical location but a symbol of power, wealth, and ultimately, the consequences of arrogance and misuse of power. The story of Lanka serves as a moral lesson about the importance of virtue, justice, and the ultimate triumph of good over evil. The island’s beauty and grandeur are often contrasted with the moral depravity of its ruler, highlighting the theme that external splendor cannot compensate for internal corruption. Even after Ravana's defeat, Lanka's legacy continues to be intertwined with themes of dharma, karma, and the cyclical nature of good and evil in Hindu mythology.
subtitle: Golden City of the Rakshasas

keyMyths.title: Sita's Abduction
keyMyths.description: Ravana abducts Sita and takes her to Lanka, leading to Rama's quest to rescue her. This act triggers the epic war between Rama and Ravana.

keyMyths.title: The Battle of Lanka
keyMyths.description: The epic battle between Rama's army and Ravana's forces takes place in Lanka. Hanuman plays a crucial role in the war, displaying immense strength and devotion.

sources.author: Valmiki
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Lanka and the events that transpired there.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Lanka and the events that transpired there.

sources.author: Tulsidas
sources.description: A popular retelling of the Ramayana, offering another perspective on Lanka and its significance.
